title: Add Internet Identity login/logout to Pulse

requirements.summary: Add Internet Identity login/logout button to navigation sidebar and mobile tab bar
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Log In' button is visible in the navigation bar when the user is not authenticated.
Clicking 'Log In' triggers the Internet Identity authentication flow via the existing `useInternetIdentity` hook.
After successful login, the navigation bar replaces the 'Log In' button with an authenticated user indicator (avatar/principal truncated) and a 'Log Out' button.
Clicking 'Log Out' calls the `logout` function and returns the UI to the unauthenticated state.
The login/logout button is accessible on both the desktop sidebar and mobile bottom tab bar layouts.
The login UI matches the existing coral/rose theme and respects dark/light mode.

file_operations.description: Import and call the `useInternetIdentity` hook to access `isAuthenticated`, `identity`, `login`, and `logout`. In the desktop sidebar, add a new section below the theme toggle that shows a 'Log In' button when unauthenticated (using the hook's `login` function) or displays the truncated principal (first 5 + '...' + last 3 characters) with a 'Log Out' button when authenticated (using the hook's `logout` function). Mirror this login/logout UI in the mobile bottom tab bar by adding a new tab item or inline control. Style the buttons using existing Tailwind classes to match the coral/rose theme and respect dark/light mode.

requirements.summary: Display logged-in user's Internet Identity principal in navigation and Profile page header when authenticated
acceptanceCriteria: When authenticated, the Profile navigation link shows the user's principal (e.g., first 5 + '...' + last 3 chars) or a generic 'You' label.
When unauthenticated, the Profile page continues to display mock data as before.
No changes to immutable hook files (`useInternetIdentity.ts`, `useActor.ts`).

file_operations.description: Import and call the `useInternetIdentity` hook to check `isAuthenticated` and `identity`. When the user is authenticated, replace the 'My Profile' nav link label with a truncated principal (first 5 + '...' + last 3 characters) or a generic 'You' label. When unauthenticated, keep the existing 'My Profile' label. Ensure this logic applies to both the desktop sidebar and the mobile bottom tab bar Profile link.

Import and call the `useInternetIdentity` hook to access `isAuthenticated` and `identity`. When authenticated, update the profile header to display the truncated principal (first 5 + '...' + last 3 characters) in place of the mock username. When unauthenticated, continue displaying the existing mock user data from `mockData.ts`. Apply this conditional rendering to the username display in the header section without modifying the avatar, bio, or stats components.
